codeReview.summary: The fix partially addresses the prior failure by auto-creating obj_desc_quant/obj_desc_cat rows and by extending the trigger-ordering tests to exercise values_quant and values_cat. However, it still does not guarantee the required (object, desc_inst) prerequisite row in obj_desc_inst: _ensure_obj_desc_inst exits as soon as any row exists for the object, while values_quant and values_cat require the specific (object, desc_inst) pair. As a result, deep_upsert can still fail for objects that already have a different obj_desc_inst mapping.

issues.description: The fix still leaves VAL-UPSERT-009 incomplete. _ensure_obj_desc_inst() checks only whether any obj_desc_inst row exists for the object and returns immediately if one does (generic_ingest.py:785-789), but values_quant and values_cat require the exact (object, desc_inst) pair via FOREIGN KEY (object, desc_inst) REFERENCES obj_desc_inst (object, desc_inst) (sql/tables.sql:565-566 and 644-645). If an object already has obj_desc_inst for some other descriptor, deep_upsert(values_quant/values_cat, ...) will still skip creating the needed row and can fail on the composite FK.

The new trigger-ordering tests only cover objects with no obj_desc_inst rows at all (test/test_deep_upsert.py:538-552 and 635-649). They never exercise the still-broken case where the object already has an obj_desc_inst row for a different desc_inst, so the suite does not verify the feature requirement to auto-create the specific (object, desc_inst) prerequisite row.

summary: Fail: the fix adds obj_desc_quant/obj_desc_cat auto-creation and broader tests, but deep_upsert still skips creating the required (object, desc_inst) obj_desc_inst row when the object already has a different descriptor mapping, so the original blocking correctness issue is not fully resolved.
